article.title: AltSendme: The Secure, Serverless Way to Share Files Directly
article.body_markdown:
---
title: AltSendme: The Secure, Serverless Way to Share Files Directly
published: True
tags: peertopeer, file, open, security
---

## Quick Summary: 📝
AltSendme is a free, open-source, and privacy-focused tool for direct peer-to-peer file transfers. It enables users to send any file or folder of any size across the globe without relying on cloud storage, ensuring end-to-end encryption and no account requirements.

## Key Takeaways: 💡

* ✅ AltSendme enables direct, peer-to-peer file transfers, bypassing cloud servers entirely.

* ✅ It offers end-to-end encryption and requires no accounts or personal information for maximum privacy and security.

* ✅ Transfers are fast, reliable, resumable, and can handle files or directories of any size, anywhere in the world.

* ✅ Powered by the Iroh networking stack, it includes advanced features like NAT traversal for seamless connections.

* ✅ As a free and open-source solution, it provides transparency and eliminates common cloud-sharing limitations.


## Project Statistics: 📊
* ⭐ **Stars:** 5548
* 🍴 **Forks:** 324
* ❗ **Open Issues:** 11


## Tech Stack: 💻
- ✅ TypeScript


Tired of wrestling with cloud storage limits, privacy concerns, and slow uploads just to share a file with a colleague or friend? We've all been there, uploading sensitive documents to third-party servers, hoping they're secure, only to find the download link expires or the service limits file size. What if there was a better way, a truly direct way to send files without any intermediaries?

Enter AltSendme, a fantastic open-source project that's changing how we think about file transfers. Imagine sending files directly from your computer to someone else's, anywhere in the world, without them ever touching a cloud server. That's exactly what AltSendme delivers, leveraging cutting-edge peer-to-peer networking to create a secure, private, and incredibly efficient file-sharing experience.

The magic behind AltSendme is surprisingly simple for the user. You just drag and drop your file or folder into the application. AltSendme then generates a unique, one-time "ticket" – think of it like a temporary, secure key. You share this ticket with your recipient via any communication method you prefer – chat, email, text. Your friend pastes that ticket into their AltSendme app, and boom! The transfer begins directly between your devices. No sign-ups, no personal information required, just a straightforward, encrypted connection.

Under the hood, AltSendme is powered by Iroh, a modern modular networking technology designed for robust peer-to-peer communication. This means you get features like end-to-end encryption (using QUIC + TLS 1.3 for top-tier security), ensuring your data is always protected and private. It also handles the complex stuff like NAT traversal and hole-punching, so transfers work seamlessly whether you're on the same local network or across continents, even behind tricky firewalls, with encrypted relay fallback if a direct connection isn't immediately possible.

Developers, this project is a game-changer for several reasons. First, the emphasis on privacy and security is paramount. No more worrying about data breaches on third-party servers; your files go straight from source to destination. Second, the performance is phenomenal, capable of saturating multi-gigabit connections, which means lightning-fast transfers for even the largest files or directories. Transfers are also resumable, so if your connection drops, it picks up right where it left off. And because it's free and open-source, you have complete transparency and control, with no hidden costs or size limits. For anyone building applications that involve file sharing or needs a robust, secure way to move data, understanding and potentially integrating with the underlying Iroh technology, or simply using AltSendme as a reliable utility, offers immense value. It’s a powerful demonstration of what modern P2P networking can achieve, freeing us from the constraints and compromises of traditional cloud-based file sharing.

## Learn More: 🔗
[View the Project on GitHub](https://github.com/tonyantony300/alt-sendme)
